business_ideas.idea: After.com: cremation pre-planning service capitalizing on rising cremation preference trend
Lice testing/treatment mobile service (multiple brands: LiceLifters, HairHelpers, HairAngels)
Covet Maine: direct-to-stylist hair extension marketplace with community events
Roundtable.ai: AI-powered survey simulation using LLMs to model human responses
Restaurant dessert menu gamification: interactive buttons for ordering decisions

predictions.text: Cremation preference will continue rising, creating $X billion service industry
Lice services can franchise nationally, following current multi-location model

dynamics: Sam and Shaan discuss business trends observed from personal experiences and research. Sam focuses on identifying single-chart business opportunities and micro-hustles. Shaan shares personal anecdotes (restaurant owner Brisha, lice service discovery). Heavy emphasis on finding overlooked niches and implementing psychological/behavioral optimization.
frameworks: One-chart businesses: single trend visualization can indicate entire business opportunity
Micro-hustles: small psychological/operational tweaks that compound to business success
Triple A Locksmith principle: survive by doing one small thing better in obvious ways
Direct-to-professional model: bypass retail intermediaries for higher margins
Behavioral economics in operations: small choice architecture changes drive major revenue gains
learnings: Business creation thesis can be derived from single trend chart (cremation, genome cost, college prices)
Micro-hustles compound: restaurant waiter's strategic dessert menu framing increases ticket size 40%
Overlooked service businesses (lice treatment, cremation) can generate $10-50K monthly with minimal overhead
Direct distribution to professionals (stylists) provides better unit economics than consumer-facing apps
AAA Locksmith thinking: solve obvious problem within existing job to bootstrap entrepreneurship mindset

banger_quotes.quote: One chart alone can be your entire business plan.
Restaurant owner energy is the one percent feeling, not the 99 percent hard work.
I want the engineer with just 10-15% of their mind on the micro-hustle.
You literally become a different person when you play games in your head every day.

numbers_dropped: After.com TAM: entire global population eventually
Lice service pricing: $300-600 per family visit
Lice service monthly revenue: tens of thousands per operator
Covet Maine year 1 revenue: $4M with 6 employees
Covet Maine year 4 revenue: tens of millions
Roundtable.ai survey simulation: 52% adoption rate (e-bikes among Tesla owners vs 28% general)
Restaurant dessert ticket lift: ~$40 per table through behavioral design
Disco Frisco Taxi revenue: ~$1K per night on New Year's (informal experiment)
notable: Introduces 'one-chart business' framework: identifying $100M+ opportunities from single trend visualization; extensive focus on micro-hustles as pathway from employment to entrepreneurship; Covet Maine as case study of community-driven B2B marketplace reaching tens of millions revenue.
